info.title: Fenergo Nebula Document Management Command
info.description: Command API to manage the documents uploaded from FenX app. The documents can be linked with Entity Id, Journey Id and Document Requirement

post.summary: Create acceptable document set model
post.description:
• Creates a new acceptable document set with a first draft version.
  • The new version initializes with the provided data, has the default status of draft and the author as the only required signee.


Required permissions:
Following permissions are required: DocumentConfigurationEdit

parameters.name: X-TENANT-ID
parameters.description: The UiD of the tenant representing organization

202.description: Acceptable document set model created successfully

400.description: Invalid request

500.description: An exception occurred while processing the command

401.description: User is not authorized to perform this request

messages.message: Access denied. Following permissions are required: Permission1, Permission2

post.summary: Save document metadata and generate the URL to upload the file - CreateDocumentModel
post.description:
This method will create the document model metadata and generate a signed URL to allow the user to upload the actual file directly in the AWS S3 bucket. The method will return an object with some basic information to show the result to the user in the UI like status, document requirement and document type. The response also contains the URL to upload the file in the S3 bucket.

Required permissions:
At least one of the following permissions is required: DocumentManagementCreate, DocumentManagementEdit

parameters.name: X-TENANT-ID
parameters.description: The UiD of the tenant representing organization

requestBody.description: The upload document request

202.description: An instance of the Fenergo.Nebula.DocumentManagement.Command.Application.DTO.CreateSignedUrlToUploadFileResponse class with some metadata that represent the file uploaded

400.description: Bad request. The request has missing/invalid values

401.description: User is not authorized to perform this request

messages.message: Access denied. Following permissions are required: Permission1, Permission2

post.summary: Save temporary document metadata and generate the URL to upload the file for multiple Document Requirements
post.description:
This method will create temporary document model metadata and generate a signed URL to allow the user to upload temporary file directly in the AWS S3 bucket. When multiple document Requirements need to be fulfilled Important: The returned `SignedUrlToUpload` is a presigned S3 URL that includes the `x-amz-tagging` header in its signature. When uploading the file via HTTP PUT to this URL, the caller must include the following header exactly as shown: ```x-amz-tagging: TTL=enabled``` Omitting this header or providing a different value will result in a `403 SignatureDoesNotMatch` error from S3, because the header is part of the presigned URL's HMAC signature.

Required permissions:
At least one of the following permissions is required: DocumentManagementCreate, DocumentManagementEdit

parameters.name: X-TENANT-ID
parameters.description: The UiD of the tenant representing organization

requestBody.description: The upload multiple document requirements request

202.description: An instance of the Fenergo.Nebula.DocumentManagement.Command.Application.DTO.CreateSignedUrlToUploadFileMultipleRequirementsResponse class with some metadata that represent the files uploaded

400.description: Bad request. The request has missing/invalid values

messages.message: Not found. Document model or Document Requirement model with given id does not exist

- INFECTED_FILE (Used along with Status Infected.Antivirus result)
- INVALID_MIME_TYPE (Invalid file, MIME type/extension doesn't match with the file)
- CLIENT_SIDE_ERROR (An error happened on the client side while uploading the file)
- SERVER_SIDE_ERROR (Internal server error during file processing)
